name: Stem Cell Rejuvenation Coverage Repair

scope.question: Which known source-completeness gaps for Stem Cell Rejuvenation need repair, and does resolving them change the coverage verdict or public boundaries?

summary: Coverage repair resolved the high-priority active-registry gap by staging hDNAgr therapy, NCT06877377, and no-results trial-watch records for the Stem Cell Rejuvenation track. It also repaired recent-review triangulation with PMID 42216078 and recorded older withdrawn/no-results registries plus adjacent 15-PGDH/MMP9 animal sources as excluded or taxonomy-boundary context. The staged outlook keeps the track at low-confidence human_biomarker_signal with steady momentum: the additions improve registry, review, safety, and boundary coverage, but no result-bearing targeted human stem-cell rejuvenation evidence is available.

materiality_decision.rationale: The repair changes the public source map and outlook support map by adding hDNAgr no-results registry context and a current HSC aging review, but it does not change stage, momentum, read firmness, or rating-change criteria. Public staging is warranted because the active-registry and review gaps were the reason the latest coverage assessment remained thin.

search_log.query: NCT06877377, NCT03473288, and NCT03754842 direct registry checks
search_log.summary: Confirmed that NCT06877377 is a 60-participant not-yet-recruiting randomized blinded placebo-controlled hDNAgr aging registry with no posted results; NCT03473288 is a withdrawn zero-enrollment exercise/satellite-cell registry; NCT03754842 is a completed 32-participant NR/pterostilbene muscle-regeneration registry with no posted results and stronger NAD-overlap than stem-cell-rejuvenation specificity.

search_log.query: Recent review triangulation for stem cell aging rejuvenation, hematopoietic stem cell aging review, and muscle stem cell aging rejuvenation review from 2024-01-01 through 2026-06-09
search_log.summary: The broad review query returned direct HSC and adjacent stem-cell aging reviews. PMID 42216078 was selected because it directly reviews HSC aging mechanisms and rejuvenation strategies; PMID 42209503 and PMID 42183054 were kept as adjacent mitochondrial/stromal context rather than staged public support.

Confirmed that PMID 42228536 and PMID 42220642 are adjacent animal tissue-repair sources, PMID 42249716 is neural-crest review context, PMID 42216078 is directly relevant HSC review context, and PMID 42209503/42183054 are adjacent mitochondrial and bone-marrow stromal context.

trial_watch_checks.summary: Staged as no-results trial-watch context for hDNAgr therapy because it is relevant to targeted human HSC-aging intervention coverage but has no posted results.
Withdrawn zero-enrollment exercise/satellite-cell registry; recorded as excluded context rather than staged public evidence.
Completed no-results NR/pterostilbene muscle-regeneration registry; kept as NAD-overlap and satellite-cell context rather than staged under Stem Cell Rejuvenation in this repair.

excluded_sources.title: Rejuvenation of Aged Muscle Stem Cells Through Exercise
excluded_sources.reason: Withdrawn registry with zero actual enrollment. It confirms failed or non-started human satellite-cell exercise context but does not merit a public finding beyond the staged hDNAgr registry and existing exercise meta-analysis.

excluded_sources.title: Effect of Nicotinamide Riboside and Pterostilbene Supplementation on Muscle Regeneration in Elderly Humans
excluded_sources.reason: Completed no-results registry with satellite-cell endpoints, but the intervention is NR/pterostilbene and belongs closer to NAD/redox overlap unless posted results appear.

excluded_sources.title: 15-PGDH inhibition promotes muscle repair and strength recovery during GLP-1 receptor agonist-induced weight loss.
excluded_sources.reason: Mouse obesity and injury-repair context with muscle stem-cell function and strength endpoints; adjacent to tissue repair and niche biology but not a direct aged-human stem-cell rejuvenation result.

excluded_sources.title: In vivo circRNA-engineered macrophages mediate localized MMP9 neutralization to rejuvenate aged bone.
excluded_sources.reason: Aged-mouse skeletal repair and macrophage/MMP9 immunotherapy context with stem-cell senescence readouts; useful for taxonomy review but closer to immune modulation, niche support, or tissue repair than the current stem-cell-rejuvenation public claim.

excluded_sources.title: Mitochondrial drivers of stem cell aging and inflammaging.
excluded_sources.reason: Review-level mitochondrial and NAD-linked stem-cell aging context; useful for cross-track boundaries but not selected over the more direct HSC aging review.

excluded_sources.title: Impact of aging on CD146+ mesenchymal stromal cells-mediated regulation of bone marrow CD34+ hematopoietic stem/progenitor cell mobilization.
excluded_sources.reason: Human in vitro bone-marrow stromal/HSPC mobilization model; decision-relevant to niche support and observational human-cell context but not a targeted in vivo rejuvenation therapy.

notes: The staged public change repairs source completeness and taxonomy boundaries; it does not claim hDNAgr efficacy.
NCT06877377 was staged because it is the only targeted aging HSC registry found in the repair pass and was the high-priority coverage gap.
PMID 42216078 was staged because it directly reviews HSC aging and rejuvenation strategies, filling the recent-review triangulation gap.
Adjacent animal sources were not staged because they would blur the boundary with niche support, immune modulation, and tissue repair.
next_actions: Run source-fidelity, interpretation/forecast, safety-limitations, and taxonomy-mapping reviews for the staged repair bundle.
If approved and published, return Stem Cell Rejuvenation to ordinary surveillance with special watch on NCT06877377 recruitment or posted results.
